name: Zuppa D'aglio - Garlic Soup
recipeIngredient: 1 medium Potato, Peeled And Cut Up
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut two of the garlic heads in half across and remove the papery outer skin. Place these cut sides down on a very hot surface (griddle or black iron skillet) until they are black. Peel all the garlic completely.
2. Heat olive oil in a large saucepan and saute onions, celery, and garlic until lightly browned at the edges. Add wine and bring to a boil.
3. Sprinkle flour over the pot and stir thoroughly, avoiding browning. Add chicken stock, 1/2 gallon water, and potato. Whisk well and bring to a boil. Reduce to a rapid simmer and cook for about an hour and 15 minutes.
4. Strain solids from soup, puree them in a blender or food processor with enough broth to aid blending. Return puree to soup and restore boiling. Add whipping cream, basil, thyme, salt, pepper, and Tabasco.
5. Heat extra virgin olive oil in a skillet very hot. Toast garlic slivers until browned on the outside.
6. Serve soup in bowls garnished with roasted garlic slivers. Float toasted, garlic-buttered slice of French bread on top. Serves eight to twelve.
Suggested wine: Marinasco
